name: glob_tool
summary: Find files in the working directory tree by glob pattern.
Returns matching paths sorted by modification time, newest first; capped at 200 results.
constraints: Results are paths relative to the search base, one per line.
Use this when you need to enumerate files by name or extension before deciding what to read.
The base directory is resolved against the agent's working directory; absolute paths in `path` escape it.
anti_patterns: To list a single directory's entries non-recursively — use `list_directory_tool`.
For open-ended exploration that needs multiple search rounds — delegate to `agent_tool`.
cautions: IMPORTANT: results are capped at 200; if you suspect more matches, narrow the pattern rather than asking the agent to paginate.
output: Plain text. One matching file path per line, relative to `path`. Sorted by modification time, newest first. Up to 200 lines. Empty string when nothing matches.

pattern.description: Glob pattern, evaluated relative to `path` (e.g. `**/*.rs`, `src/*.toml`). Required.

path.description: Base directory to search under (default: `.`, i.e. the agent's working directory).
